title: Comparing a list to True
problem: Set label to 'has items' when the list has something in it, otherwise 'empty'.

trace.note: `items == True` is False. A non-empty list is 'truthy', but it is NOT equal to the bool True — so this comparison fails and we fall to the else.
trace.checkpoint_prompt: items is [3, 8] and the test is `items == True`. Which branch runs — does label become 'has items' or 'empty'?

trace.note: label ends as 'empty', even though the list clearly has items. Comparing to True is not the same as asking whether the list is truthy.
